cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the answer to this question based on the provided passage. Let me read through the passage again carefully to make sure I understand what it's saying about the World Bank study from 1974. The question is asking, according to the World Bank study in 1974, what was the outcome of economic growth in some backward countries. The options are (A) benefit to a third, (B) benefit to two thirds, (C) little benefit, (D) no benefit. Looking at the passage, there's a section that says: "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." Wait, that wording is a bit confusing. Let me parse it again. The sentence says that decades of rapid growth "has been of little benefit to a third of their population." So, the growth was of little benefit to a third. That would mean that a third of the population didn't benefit much, but the other two-thirds might have? Or does it mean that the growth's benefit was only reaching a third, implying that most didn't benefit? Wait, the sentence structure is: "more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." The phrasing is a bit tricky. Let's break it down. The study concluded that the growth had been of little benefit to a third (i.e., 33%) of the population. That suggests that only a third received the benefit, while the rest (two-thirds) didn't. But the options are about what the growth brought. The question is about what the study said the growth brought. The next sentence says: "The World Bank study showed that increase in GNP per capita did not promise important improvements in such development indicators as nutrition, health, and education. Although GNP per capita did indeed rise, its benefit came down to only a small part of the population." So, the study found that even though GNP per capita increased (growth), the benefits were only for a small part of the population. The passage earlier said "little benefit to a third of their population." So in the 1974 study, the conclusion was that growth in developing countries had been of little benefit to a third.
